issues.title: [iOS][iOS 17] IME shows at the wrong place for text input on iPhone + iOS 17 + hardware keyboard
issues.body: ### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create an empty project with a text field (or just use flutter/dev/integration_tests/ios_platform_view_tests 2. Connect hardware keyboard on device (or CMD+K on simulator) 3. Change input to IME language (e.g. Chinese) 4. Type and observe the issue Verified that this only happens to iPhone with iOS 17 + hardware keyboard ### Expected results The IME should be positioned right below to the text input ### Actual results The IME is positioned at the wrong place ### Code sample NA ### Screenshots or Video Originally, it's on top left corner ![phone ios 17, main branch](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/41930132/3e8725e1-2cc4-4136-a59a-fd4c403eac5c) Then we fixed an auto correction issue (https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44779), now it shows close to the right place, but still a bit off: ### Logs NA ### Flutter Doctor output NA

issues.title: The autofill dialog can appear in the wrong place
comments: CC @LongCatIsLooong

Hi @justinmc I've looked into this. This somehow seems to be specific to Gboard. I can reproduce it with Gboard 9.4 but cannot reproduce the issue on older Gboard 8.0. However, since Gboard 9.8, (which was released in late 2020) It no longer shows autofill dialog, and instead, autofill suggestions are shown in a fixed place above the keyboard. Regarding iOS, it too always shows autofill suggestions in a fixed place on the keyboard. | Gboard 8.0 | Gboard 9.4 | Gboard 9.8 (released in 2020) | | --------------- | --------------- | --------------- | | | | Considering this only reproduces on certain Gboard versions older than 2 years and no longer even shows autofill dialog Gboard versions since 2 years. Do we still want to fix this?

issues.title: [TextFormField] Hold SuffixIcon triggers the focusNode on web and desktop platforms.
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce:

Create a TextFormField and add an IconButton in the suffix (e.g., suffixIcon).

Run the app and navigate to the screen with the TextFormField.

Try clicking the IconButton inside the suffix.

Notice that the TextFormField receives focus when the suffix IconButton is clicked.

Expected Behavior:
Clicking the suffix IconButton should not trigger the focus event on the TextFormField.

Actual Behavior:
Clicking the suffix IconButton triggers the focus on the TextFormField.



### Expected results

FocusNode listener only if you click on the field

### Actual results

FocusNode listener triggers if you only hold

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() {
  runApp(const MaterialApp(home: MyApp()));
}

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MyApp> createState() => _MyAppState();
}

class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> {
  final TextEditingController _textFormController = TextEditingController();
  final FocusNode _focusNode = FocusNode();
  

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Container(
          alignment: Alignment.center,
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(6),
          width: 500,
          child: TextFormField(
            controller: _textFormController,
            focusNode: _focusNode,
            
            keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
            maxLines: null,
            style: GoogleFonts.spaceMono(
              fontSize: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium!.fontSize,
            ),
            decoration: InputDecoration(
                border: OutlineInputBorder(),
                suffixIcon: SizedBox(
                  width: 80,
                  child: Row(
                    children: [
                      IconButton(
                        onPressed: () {
                          _textFormController.clear();
                        },
                        icon: Icon(Icons.close),
                      ),
                      IconButton(
                        onPressed: () {
                        },
                        icon: Icon(Icons.person),
                      )
                    ],
                  ),
                )),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>

The error always occurs when the BoxContraints width of a Text element is 0, so it is not going to be drawn to the screen, but maybe some other place tries to draw it. I am sorry don't know more than that. 

The way in which a text element is set to 0 is:

```
Row(children: [Expanded(child: Text("some")), Text("text")])]
```

When the size of the box is squished the second child doesnt compress so the first child disappears, when that happens the Texts BoxConstraints are 0, and  the error occurs.

issues.title: VoiceOver rotor actions limited to text fields with active keyboard focus
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

As I mentioned in #151029 :
Suppose there are two text fields on an interface.
1. Double-tap to activate the first text field, and the keyboard pops up.
2. Then, use VoiceOver to focus on the second text field. Note: just focus on it, don't double-tap.
3. Finally, use the rotor to perform a paste operation.
Native behavior: The text will be pasted into the second text field.
Flutter behavior: Unable to perform the paste operation.
In other words, the native rotor operation targets the text field that VoiceOver is focused on.
However, Flutter rotor operation only targets the text field that has the keyboard activated.

Please use the latest master version for testing.

### Expected results

The text will be pasted into the second text field.

### Actual results

Unable to perform the paste operation.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Two Text Fields')),
        body: Column(
          children: [
            TextField(),
            TextField(),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

### Flutter Doctor output

<details open><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
Flutter (Channel master, 3.26.0-1.0.pre.97

</details>

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch provided sample in web environment
2. Use tab key to traverse between inputs

The default [behavior](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TraversalEdgeBehavior.html) used on web by MaterialApp is `leaveFlutterView`.

### Expected results

Input should traverse between inputs before the flutter app, then between the inputs within the flutter app, then after the flutter app.

Input cycles between the flutter app and a single input following the flutter app.

Hello everyone,

I've encountered this issue in my project as well. Has anyone come across a temporary workaround or fix for this problem while the official solution is being developed? Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.

issues.title: [Proposal] Expose `padding` for multi-line `TextField`
issues.body:
## Use case

I have a Flutter desktop app with a full bleed `TextField` with infinite `maxLine` contraint. The text comes too close to the edges of the window, and I'd like to pad the content. 

I can pad the `TextField` on the left and right with a `Padding` widget, but I can't pad the top and bottom without creating an unsightly top and bottom gap during text scroll.

## Proposal

Please expose something akin to [`BoxScrollView`'s `padding`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/BoxScrollView/padding.html) property to multi-line `TextField`.

issues.title: [iPadOS] Stage Manager has incorrect inset when in top corner
issues.body:
## Requirements to Reproduce
* Must be using one of the following devices: 
  * iPad Pro 12.9-inch (3rd generation and later)
  * iPad Pro 11-inch (1st generation and later)
  * iPad Air (5th generation)
* If using a simulator, Xcode version must be at least 14.1 
* Must have Stage Manager enabled (Settings < Home Screen & Multitasking < Stage Manager = On)
* Must be in Portrait orientation

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
1. Execute `flutter run` on the [code sample](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104689#issuecomment-1139253289).
2. Grab the handle in the bottom right corner and drag inward to make it as small as possible.
3. Click the three dots at the top of the app and select `Add Another Window` and then select a different app
4. Grab the handle in the bottom right corner and drag inward to make other app as small as possible too.
5. Grab three dots at top of the flutter app and drag it up so it's closer to the top of the screen.
6. Click the input field in the flutter app to open the keyboard.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Expect the flutter app would have no inset below the input field (so it should look the same as it did when the keyboard was closed).

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

Very small inset below the input field.

![simulator_screenshot_DC237399-6E33-47E8-BC40-0C6329261675](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15619084/208488102-2f0f4eee-0f8d-4b60-a1ed-d58494493493.png)

## Notes
For some reason, when it's in this size and position in Stage Manager, the info provided by `UIKeyboardFrameEndUserInfoKey` does not seem accurate.

For example, with an iPad Pro 11 inch:
The screen's dimensions are 834 x 1194.
If the app is full screen, the keyboard's dimensions are 834 x 340. And its origin position is (0, 854). This is as expected. The origin.y is 340 (the keyboard's height) from the bottom of the screen (1194 - 854 = 340).
If the app is top right/left (like screenshot above), the keyboard's dimensions are 834 x 340. However, its origin position is (0, 661). This is not expected. For some reason, the origin.y is wrong for this use case.
If the app is middle or bottom right or left, the keyboard's frame is the same as if it were full screen.

Then please update the documents or else more clear to warn the users. And I don't think the behavior is normal since it's using the intermediate letters which should not consider 'input' texts in any way.

> Just to clear that, did you see examples in the docs where a controller is created inside of the build method?

I just not realized the issue is about the controller.

Actually I'm not the only one met the same issue:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/27012

That issue is closed with a bad solution - just not to use the onChanged method. Which is not acceptable for me.
It took me hours to find the reason - as I mentioned the solution before. 
If I could realize the issue is where the controller is, I may not spend hours to solve the issue.

Thanks a lot for this feedback I already tagged this issue for more documentation on this. It will stay open
Whatever the problem is, it seems to be similar to this: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63500276/swiftui-textfield-behaves-weirdly-when-i-bind-its-value-cant-type-chinese-occ
@Trannosaur, just to confirm, you're still seeing this? We had a somewhat similar issue on Android, though IIRC that was device-specific to Samsung keyboards -- @GaryQian may have thoughts.

Sorry you can probably safely ignore my comment. While what I was seeing, it is literally happening *today*, unless Flutter is using Swift's TextField when on iOS devices it probably is just a coincidence. I found that it simply is that Swift doesn't support any IME with intermediate modes if you're binding it. (Chinese, Japanese, etc.)

CC @LongCatIsLooong to investigate possibly adding docs to TextEditingController. There is a sentence in the docs for GlobalKey that warns against this. One way or another we should try to help people avoid this footgun if we can.

I don't think there is a problem with creating TextEditingController in `build` (although it's probably not the best practice to have side effects in `build`). The problem is that the sample implementation resets the selection / composing region info when it recreates the controller. The sample implementation does leak controllers and that should be caught be the leak detector.

issues.title: [Windows/Linux/MacOS]: Proposal to enable simulation for text input
issues.body:
## Environment
**Attention: Windows-Dev** 💻 (but also asking for any other desktop platform (Linux/MacOS)

## Use case

I want to be able to simulate keyDownEvents in production like in https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/flutter_test/simulateKeyDownEvent.html

Requirements: **There is no hardware-keyboard available (!)**, but we're simulating an on-screen keyboard (and want to style it here and there differently). When tapping on the on-screen keyboard, i want to simulate hardware keystrokes.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7249645/154674386-6c06d3cb-b8c1-420d-89fa-cea387997db9.png)


## Proposal

Provide a function similar to e.g. `KeyEventSimulator.simulateKeyDownEvent(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyA);` 


## What i tried so far:
    RawKeyboard.instance.handleRawKeyEvent(RawKeyDownEvent(
                      data: RawKeyEventDataWindows(
                          keyCode: 65,
                          scanCode: 30,
                          characterCodePoint: 97,
                          modifiers: 0)));
                  RawKeyboard.instance.handleRawKeyEvent(RawKeyUpEvent(
                      data: RawKeyEventDataWindows(
                          keyCode: 65,
                          scanCode: 30,
                          characterCodePoint: 0,
                          modifiers: 0)));
                          
                          
It actually `emits the same events as typing on my hardware keyboard`, however, nothing happens if e.g. a TextFormField is selected. If I type it with my hardware-keyboard, everything works as intended.
comments: It does neither work on production nor in debug. Actually, I'd like to have it enabled on any mode.

> Please check [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/96022) similar issue and underlying comments and see if they help to achieve your use case.

Did you get a chance to go through this linked issue to see if any of the underlying comments in it makes sense for your case ?
Related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/76072
Thank for further details.
/cc @gspencergoog

Correct, it's not going to work to just call `RawKeyboard.handleRawKeyEvent`, since the actual text input is a cooperation between the engine and the framework, and you're just simulating the framework side when you do that. Because input method editors need to provide an edit stream rather than key events, we need to get the text input through the engine.

For your use case, have you thought of just calling the relevant Windows APIs to simulate key events, using Dart's FFI? The [win32](https://pub.dev/packages/win32) package can help a lot with this. Then all of the correct things will happen with the events.

cc @dkwingsmt

@gspencergoog Thanks for your response. I like the idea and will definitely try out the [win32 package](https://pub.dev/packages/win32).

However, I think it would really complete the flutter input-mechanics if a full-fletched simulation of hardware-inputs are an option.

Why? Because my next question would be: How do I do it on MacOS / Linux? It will be the same question and would require more `ffi` etc. I don't have anything against `ffi`, but it bites me sometimes and is quite a hassle when building with a single code base. Actually, if you start to manage certain platform-specific libraries for Windows/MacOS/Linux - what is the point of having a single code base? My paradigm and understanding of flutter is: Write code once, run everywhere. Implementing a virtual-keyboard-overlay for each platform does not follow that mindset.

I would really appreciate the option to work on an `On-Screen Keyboard` for every platform without worrying of any c-compiled libraries.

Would it be technically possible?

Yes, it's technically possible.

And it would be nice, I agree, and would actually help our testing infrastructure too, but our resources are limited, and this is not a use case that is often requested, so other tasks would take priority for some time before we got to this.

@gspencergoog Cool, I don't want to rush anything, but I'm **VERY glad** to hear that. Thanks for your response!

Keep on going, still loving the product.

I agree that it's important for many applications to forge events. But make it work exactly like native takes more than just framework. Each OS has its own range of support for different types of events, especially Win32 has an extremely complicated protocol. 

But in the end, all keyboard effects come down to key events and text input. Maybe we should just provide a way to forge text input changes.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

issues.title: Add Callbacks for Clipboard-Related Events (Paste, Cut, Copy, etc.)
comments:
@hellohuanlin I'm interested in your thoughts on this one. Is it possible on the iOS platform side to know when the user has pressed one of these buttons?

The most critical thing here is probably the fact that this could trigger the "paste" notification again (the original motivation for SystemContextMenu). If Flutter is intercepting the Paste in the context menu, then we might trigger the notification that the app pasted from the clipboard.

> Is it possible on the iOS platform side to know when the user has pressed one of these buttons?

Yes, when the button is pressed, the corresponding `cut/copy/paste` function will be called on the engine, and we should be able to pipe that info to the framework. 

> The most critical thing here is probably the fact that this could trigger the "paste" notification again (the original motivation for SystemContextMenu). If Flutter is intercepting the Paste in the context menu, then we might trigger the notification that the app pasted from the clipboard.

It won't trigger if we use system context menu - when `paste:` method is called, we are free to access the paste board. In fact, this is how we implement the `paste:` method in the first place - we access the paste board, and insert the pasteboard content into the text.
@hellohuanlin Hello, thank you! I am interested in looking more into this, can you point me to where the framework handles the paste method as an example?

> @hellohuanlin Hello, thank you! I am interested in looking more into this, can you point me to where the framework handles the paste method as an example? 

With native edit menu, I don't think the engine sends that info to framework. But instead, the engine sends the delta of text to framework. So what you are asking for doesn't exist (yet).

It sounds like in the iOS embedder we would need to tie into the iOS API that @hellohuanlin mentioned, then send some kind of method channel message to the framework that indicates a system context menu button was pressed and which one. This might have some overlap with custom system context menu buttons when we implement that.
Any updates? I am still waiting for some news... T.T @hellohuanlin @justinmc

issues.title: [Proposal]Tapping a virtual keyboard's next-button, when in a text-field should not switch focus to other widgets apart from TextField
issues.body:
If a `TextField` has `textInputAction: TextInputAction.next`, a right-arrow will be displayed in the virtual keyboard in the Android phone.

If next to that `TextField` there is another `TextField`, pressing that right-arrow will move focus to that other `TextField`, which is the correct expected behavior.

However, if next to that `TextField` there is, say, a button; then pressing that right-arrow will move focus to that button, which makes no sense in a phone.

I don't understand why, in phones (Android and iOS), widgets other than textfields can have focus. There's nothing we can do with that focus.

It makes perfect sense in the desktop or web, of course.

So my questions are:

1) Does it make sense that widgets other than textfields can have focus, in phones?

2) Without having to manually add `ExcludeFocus` everywhere, how do I prevent focus of non-textfields in phones, so that pressing the abovementioned right-arrow will move focus to the next text-field? 

This issue is to suggest that non-textfield widgets should detect the platform, and accept focus only for desktop/web.
comments:
> 1. Does it make sense that widgets other than textfields can have focus, in phones?

I'm no expert on the focus system, but my understanding is that this is a useful thing in part so that mobile apps can be configured to be navigable by hardware keyboards. Let's say you have a mobile app whose main purpose is to collect customer data in forms, and it's running on an iPad that's hooked up to a physical keyboard. Ideally you should be able to configure the app to have all the fields be navigable with tabs, and then at the end, be able to focus a submit button and press enter to click it.

> 2. Without having to manually add ExcludeFocus everywhere, how do I prevent focus of non-textfields in phones, so that pressing the abovementioned right-arrow will move focus to the next text-field?

I think you might be looking for `FocusNode.skipTraversal`?: https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/FocusNode/skipTraversal.html

I can think of a few other ways to get around this if you want it more immediately baked into your app's components, but my understanding is that setting `skipTraversal` to `true` on your own provided `FocusNode` will prevent it from being focused when navigating through the focus tree.

Mobile devices almost never have keyboards, so giving focus to buttons in mobile devices should never be the default behavior. But I'd agree that, more precisely, a widget should only gain focus if there is a keyboard connected to itself when it has focus, whatever keyboard it may be. Which in mobile devices means removing focus from non-textfields, unless there is a physical keyboard.

No, `FocusNode.skipTraversal` is not what I want. Having to add `FocusNode.skipTraversal` everywhere is an even greater amount of work as having to add `ExcludeFocus` everywhere. And `FocusNode.skipTraversal` still lets the non-textfields gain focus in other ways, which is not useful at all.

My point is simple: Tapping the keyboard next-button when in a text-field and having the focus end up in a button (or checkbox, or radiobutton etc) in a mobile device with no physical keyboard makes no sense. The virtual keyboard will just close, and the button will change color, get focus, but there is no way for the user to do anything with that.
Gotcha, ok understood. I'll let someone else (maybe someone actually on the team) weigh in on the framework design there 👍🏻

Hi @marcglasberg, Thanks for filing the issue. I believe there are many use cases where you want to programmatically focus on widgets, One main use case I can think of is the Accessibility feature (Talkback in Android or VoiceOver on IOS) where swiping left or right the focus shifts on different widgets and double-tap anywhere onscreen simulates a tap on the currently focused widget. Also when mobile phones are connected with other I/O devices e.g joystick or physical keyboard focus is required.
In case you don't want a particular widget or part of a widget tree to gain focus you may wrap it in [ExcludeFocus](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/ExcludeFocus-class.html), I am not sure if there is any other way apart from this.
But I believe its just a few lines of code to remove focus on widgets which wouldn't make a difference.

@maheshmnj Yes, you are right, we should be allowed to programmatically focus on widgets. 

This discussion has shown me that there are a lot of use cases where you do want those non-text-field widgets to actually get focus. I changed this issue's description to: **Tapping a VIRTUAL keyboard's next-button, when in a text-field, should move us to the next text-field.**

This is not something for the app developer to deal with. You said "In case you don't want a particular widget or part of a widget tree to gain focus"... But it's not that I want or don't want some widgets to get focus. Nobody would want a button to get focus after a next button is pressed on a virtual keyboard, because the keyboard will close, and that's a dead end. 

It remains true that 99% of the time you are in Android/iOS, the keyboard is virtual, and no accessibility feature is being used. So, most of the time, overwhelmingly, having the focus end up in a button (or checkbox, or radiobutton etc) in a mobile device with no physical keyboard makes no sense at all. 

Doing the right thing here can and should be automatic.

>  But it's not that I want or don't want some widgets to get focus. Nobody would want a button to get focus after a next button is pressed on a virtual keyboard, because the keyboard will close, and that's a dead end.

Thanks for the info @marcglasberg, This does seem like a plausible proposal. I will leave this open for further thoughts from the team.

issues.title: Preserve `SelectableText`'s selection on setState
comments:
Hi @kaboc 
This doesn't look like a bug, [setState](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/State/setState.html) rebuilds the layout, and as a result, the selection is lost.  Please correct me if I am mistaken.

@TahaTesser 
If it's not a bug, is there a non-hacky solution to update the text style without losing selection? If not, I think it means there's something that can be improved on Flutter side.

I wanted to make my package compatible with `SelectableText` in addition to `Text` because text selection is more important as Flutter is more widely used for web, but it looked impossible, which is why I filed the issue.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

You should be able to do this using [SelectionArea](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/SelectionArea-class.html) instead of SelectableText, but let me know if I'm wrong. This looks like a legitimate use case for SelectableText, but given that we are trying to replace that widget with SelectionArea where possible, supporting this hasn't been a priority.

I think I had filed this issue before `SelectionArea` was introduced. I needed a fix or workaround back then so that `SelectableText` and text decorations would go well together in one of my packages, but later on, gave up the idea of supporting it and instead recommended the users combine the package with `SelectionArea`. So it is fine for me that this won't be fixed, given `SelectableText` will be deprecated, but `SelectionArea` is still missing some features. Just hoping it becomes better soon.

Thanks for raising this.

We’re also seeing this issue across multiple iPads with physical keyboards. Many of our users rely heavily on keyboard navigation for report writing, and the inability to hold the arrow keys to move the cursor is significantly impacting usability.

This is core functionality expected across all platforms, and it’s surprising that it’s not working as expected on iPadOS. We'd really appreciate any updates or guidance on this.

Let me know if there’s anything I can provide to help with triaging or reproducing this further.

Thanks for the report. I was able to replicate the reported behavior on iPad simulator as well as on iOS simulator as shown below:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/621c52c9-d109-4ba8-892d-4313918256f4

I verified with messaging app on iPad simulator in which the cursor moves properly after pressing-holding the left arrow key:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24b7eac8-b99d-424e-8773-756d40cadef5

I also verified on Android emulator and on it, the reported behavior doesn't occur as shown below, the cursor moves properly.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/395302bf-213c-4990-b7e6-d72c425406e8


```
stable: 3.32.7
master : 3.33.0-1.0.pre-1064
```
Thanks for reporting this! I was able to reproduce this on latest master as well a ~2 year old version of Flutter, v3.10.6, so this does not appear to be a recent regression.

Hello, this is my first contribution to the Flutter project.

I'm working on a PR to address this issue.

I have a question about the implementation approach I've taken.

I implemented a solution that uses a timer to control continuous arrow key input when keys are held down.

However, I noticed that Flutter's style guide mentions that using timers is prohibited.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/main/docs/contributing/Style-guide-for-Flutter-repo.md#summary

I would like to clarify: Is using timers completely forbidden, or are there specific cases where they are acceptable?

If timers are not allowed, I understand that I would need to modify the engine-side code instead. I'm happy to adjust my approach accordingly.

@narunblog I think the style guide is referring to timeouts (see [this section](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/main/docs/contributing/Style-guide-for-Flutter-repo.md#never-check-if-a-port-is-available-before-using-it-never-add-timeouts-and-other-race-conditions)). Timers in this case should be acceptable.

That being said, I do wonder if this problem is limited to text editing or other cases that involve repeated key events (although I can't think of any). It would be best if we can investigate this problem for a bit before proceeding.

issues.title: Lenovo pen button not recognized
issues.body: I am using a lenovo pen, specifically the Lenovo Digital Pen 2, on my Lenovo IdeaPad 5 2-in-1 14AHP9 running Fedora 42 (Workstation Edition) and whenever I press the button that is normally erase it is not recognized at all by flutter. There are two buttons on the pen. One of the buttons is recognized, but the other is not. The tip of the pen is recognized. To reproduce this problem I used this code: https://github.com/CodeDoctorDE/flutter-input-demo It uses event.buttons to print out what buttons are being pressed on the pen. Pressing the pen against the screen results in event.buttons outputting a 1 (it is 0 when not touching the screen). For all of the screenshots you're going to want to look at the number on the far right. issues.title: Document intended semantics behavior on each Flutter widget - whether standalone or building block
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

**Context:** I ran into `TextField` merging its semantics into the parent, resulting into an incorrect semantics tree. This has already been filed as an issues at: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/26336.

**Issue:** One of the challenges I run into with Flutter component documentation is that it's not clear for each widget whether it is intended to be
- **standalone** (and provides `Semantics(container: true)`) or
- a **building block** (provides `Semantics`, but without `container: true`, intending to be merged with its parent)

This has to be determined by trial and error or digging to various abstractions in the code. Sometimes it's clear from the examples given, but this is confusing for cases like `IconButton` whose behavior depends on whether `theme.useMaterial3` is enabled:
* `theme.useMaterial3=true`: [emits `container: true` via another widget](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/353b8bc87d297295069ae1b47885920f07d16e9e/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/icon_button.dart#L712-L719)
* `theme.useMaterial3=false`: [doesn't emit `container: true`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/353b8bc87d297295069ae1b47885920f07d16e9e/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/icon_button.dart#L772-L775) (possibly a bug)

This means that if the app uses `theme.useMaterial3=true`, but tests have that set as false (using the default theme), then tests don't test the correct semantics behavior - I realized this pitfall by running into it last week.

One worrisome thing is that when these are set unintentionally one way or another, I'm not sure how easily they can be fixed after the fact without potential accessibility breakage (which is not visually obvious): e.g., if users use `TextField` or `IconButton` as building-blocks, but if the flutter team intend them to be standalone widgets, then fixing the bug so that they become stand-alone might break existing semantics.

### Request

Ideally, the distinction between **standalone** or **building block** would be stated up front in each Flutter widget's description.
comments:
Thanks for the report @marcianx 
Can you provide us an example (code implementation) that shows the highlighted issue, that way we can properly address this issue.

@darshankawar Could you elaborate what you need from me? I'm raising concerns about the documentation, pointed out [a related issue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/26336) that the confusion manifests as and I've pointed to various parts of the code for `IconButton` as well and the consequences of this.
Thanks for the feedback @marcianx. I was wondering if any code sample could be provided that shows the current behavior you are seeing, so that it would be useful for the team.

1. The code sample in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/128709 points to this issue in a Dart comment. Simplifying:
    ```dart
    Column(
      children: [
        RichText(text: TextSpan(text: 'Foo')),
        const TextField(maxLines: null, minLines: 8),
      ],
    )
    ```
    results in a single `SemanticsNode` with `textField: true` _and_ semantics label `Foo` instead of having separate nodes for `RichText` and `TextField`. It seems that both of these widgets lack `container: true`. As noted, the pre-existing issue for `TextField` is https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/26336. The intended behavior (whether these are intended to be stand-alone or building-block widgets) should be documented.

2. For `IconButton`'s inconsistency, I saw the instances where my team had done:
    ```dart
    CustomTooltip(
      child: IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.Add)), onPressed: () {}),
    )
    ```
    You can replace `CustomTooltip` with `Semantics(tooltip: 'Foo', child: ...)`.
    This was incorrect because with `theme.useMaterial3=true`, `IconButton` emits `Semantics(container: true, ...)` and the tooltip generates its own semantics. So I wrote a test to catch it:
    ```dart
    // Expect exactly one semantic node - the one for the button.
    expect(
      tester.semantics.simulatedAccessibilityTraversal().map((node) =>
          (node.label, button: node.hasFlag(SemanticsFlag.isButton))),
      [('Add', button: true)],
    );
    ```
    but the test didn't fail like it should have. That's because the test didn't set `theme.useMaterial3=true`, and thus `IconButton` didn't emit `container: true` on its `Semantics` and the tooltip and buttons semantics did end up merging. I fixed it by updating all the tests to set an appropriate theme so that with the incorrect use of our `CustomTooltip` above, two semantic nodes are emitted (one for the tooltip and one for the button), and the above `expect` condition fails as intended.

Again, I'm not asking about fixing these particular bugs in the widgets, but documenting the intention of **stand-alone** vs **building-block** on every Flutter widget provided, because it's critical to building with these widgets with **correct accessibility semantics**. Too often, I've run into the UI that looks right, but has serious accessibility issues because this distinction is not clear to folks building using these widgets.

Hope that helps.
@chunhtai Are you the right person for this?

yes I agree we should have more info on how different widget would participate in semantics tree, but I don't have a particular strategy to achieve this at the moment. Updating one or two widgets won't help much, we would need a more systematic solution

issues.title: TextField cursor splits Arabic characters when editing (RTL caret placed inside glyph)
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project using:
   flutter create bug

2. Replace the contents of lib/main.dart with the code sample provided below.

3. Run the app on any platform (Android / Linux / Windows).

4. Tap inside the TextField and try placing the cursor between Arabic characters.

5. The caret appears inside the visual glyph of the character instead of between characters.


### Expected results

The cursor should move between Arabic characters normally without splitting or entering inside the glyph.

Editing RTL Arabic text should work the same way it works in normal native text editors.

### Actual results

The cursor is positioned inside the Arabic character glyph instead of between characters.

This causes the character to visually split and makes text editing nearly impossible.

The issue happens even without custom fonts and on a clean Flutter setup.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  MyApp({super.key});

  final TextEditingController text = TextEditingController(
    text: """
# هذا البرنامج يقوم بطباعة الاعداد الاولية ضمن المدى المعطى له
دالة هل_اولي(عدد):
	اذا عدد < 2:
		ارجع
	اذا عدد == 2:
		اطبع(عدد)
		ارجع
	اذا ليس عدد \\ 2:
		ارجع
	لكل مقسوم في مدى(3, صحيح(\^عدد) + 1, 2):
		اذا ليس عدد \\ مقسوم:
			ارجع
	اطبع(عدد)

اطبع("*- هذا البرنامج يقوم بإيجاد الأعداد الأولية ضمن المدى المدخل له -*")

ن = صحيح(ادخل("ادخل عدد: "))

لكل ب في مدى(ن):
	هل_اولي(ب)

اطبع("تم إيجاد الاعداد الاولية ضمن العدد { ن }")
""",
  );

  @override

issues.title: Using Apple Pencil on a text field makes the full size keyboard unavailable until the textfield is tapped multiple times
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Press text field with finger, full size keyboard appears.
2. Dismiss the keyboard.
3. Press text field with Pencil, a menu appears in the corner with option to show mini keyboard.
4. Dismiss the menu.
5. Press text field with finger again, now you get the menu or mini keyboard instead of the full keyboard.

This seems to have something to do with the Pencil's Scribble feature, even though stylusHandwritingEnabled or scribbleEnabled is set to false on the text field. It only happens when Scribble is turned on in the iPad's Apple Pencil settings - if it's turned off, you always get the full size keyboard.

### Expected results

Compare to native behavior in for example Safari's search bar:
1. Press with finger, full size keyboard
2. Dismiss keyboard
3. Press with Pencil, menu in the corner
4. Dismiss menu
5. Press with finger, full size keyboard again

.

comments:
Thanks for the detailed report. I was able to replicate the reported behavior with latest sdk versions.

```
stable : 3.29.3
master : 3.32.0-1.0.pre.494
```

The main issue here is that in `SelectableRegionState._handleTouchLongPressEnd` we insert the context menu before the selection handles into the `Overlay`, we should instead insert the selection handles first so the context menu appears over the handles and not vice-versa. This should fix the issue. We should also look for other areas in `SelectableRegion` where we may be doing this.

I'm not actively working on this but hope this information is helpful. Also happy to review any pull requests fixing this.

issues.title: [macOS] CGEvent-injected Cmd+V keystrokes lose Command modifier in keyboard handler
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a simple Flutter macOS app with a TextField
2. Install any macOS app that pastes via CGEvent injection (e.g., [Raycast](https://www.raycast.com/) clipboard manager, [Wispr Flow](https://wisprflow.com/), [SuperWhisper](https://superwhisper.com/))
3. Focus the Flutter TextField
4. Use the third-party app to paste clipboard content (which simulates Cmd+V via CGEventPost)

### Expected results
Clipboard content is pasted into the TextField (same as pressing physical Cmd+V).

### Actual results
In TextField: nothing is inserted, or a lowercase "v" character appears
Physical Cmd+V works correctly in the same field
The same CGEvent-injected Cmd+V works correctly in native macOS apps (TextEdit, Safari, Notes, etc.) and even other Electron apps

### Root cause analysis
Third-party macOS apps simulate Cmd+V by:

**Copying text to the system clipboard**
Injecting a Cmd+V keystroke via CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent + CGEventPost
The CGEvent carries the correct Command modifier flag. At the NSEvent level (e.g., in performKeyEquivalent), the modifier is present. However, Flutter's internal keyboard processing pipeline loses the Command modifier state when processing synthetic CGEvent-injected keystrokes, causing the key event to be interpreted as a bare "v" character press.

This appears related to #135349 (inconsistent keyboard modifier state synchronization).

**Impact**
This breaks compatibility with an entire category of popular macOS productivity tools:

**Dictation apps**: Wispr Flow, SuperWhisper, macOS dictation alternatives
**Clipboard managers**: Raycast, Alfred, Paste, CopyClip
**Text expanders**: TextExpander, Typinator, aText
All of these rely on CGEvent injection to paste content. None work with Flutter macOS apps.

#### Workaround
Override performKeyEquivalent on the NSWindow subclass to intercept Cmd+V before it reaches Flutter's keyboard handler:

```swift
// In MainFlutterWindow.swift
override func performKeyEquivalent(with event: NSEvent) -> Bool {
    if event.type == .keyDown,
       event.modifierFlags.intersection(.deviceIndependentFlagsMask).contains(.command),
       event.charactersIgnoringModifiers == "v" {
        if NSApp.sendAction(#selector(NSText.paste(_:)), to: nil, from: self) {
            return true
        }
    }
    return super.performKeyEquivalent(with: event)
}
```

This routes the paste through the standard macOS responder chain before Flutter can lose the modifier.

**Flutter Web Note**
This also affects Flutter web running on macOS. Since defaultTargetPlatform reports TargetPlatform.macOS in the browser, any widget that uses raw keyboard event processing (e.g., packages using TextInputSource.keyboard) exhibits the same behavior — CGEvent-injected Cmd+V produces a bare "v" character.

Environment
Flutter 3.38.5
Dart 3.10.4
macOS (tested on Sonnet/Sequoia)
Affects: macOS desktop embedding + Flutter web on macOS

**Related issues**
#135349 — Inconsistent keyboard modifier state on macOS
#107037 — TextField allows key presses to be handled by shortcuts before text input engine
#55070 — Can't always paste in TextField on macOS
comments: @daverad  Thanks for the report. Could you also confirm if this reproduces on the latest stable version (`3.41.6`) of Flutter?
@saurabh-mirajkar confirmed the issue also reproduces on 3.41.6
The `triaged-ios` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-ios` label or `fyi-ios` label.

hit the same wall wiring up CGEvent paste for a macOS agent that targets flutter and a couple electron apps. the thing that unblocked us was posting the modifier state via separate flagsChanged events before the keyDown, not just setting flags on the keyDown event itself. some frameworks only update their internal modifier state from flagsChanged transitions and ignore the flags field on the keydown entirely.

the performKeyEquivalent override in the ticket is the cleaner fix for flutter specifically, but if anyone hits this in tauri or tkinter apps later the flagsChanged pattern is more portable. also worth double checking kCGEventSourceStateCombinedSessionState vs HIDSystemState on the event source, we saw flutter only honor combined session state events reliably.

issues.title: Add Theme-level option to align error text with the text field border (remove default indent/padding)
issues.body:
### Use case

Currently, when using `TextField / TextFormField`, the error text (errorText) is rendered with an indent that matches the input/hint text padding. This behavior makes the error text look visually offset from the left border of the field.

At the moment, the only way to fix this is by overriding decoration properties (error widget, custom padding, etc.) on each individual field. This is tedious and redundant in larger apps. If the padding needs to be adjusted later, developers must revisit every field manually.



### Proposal

Introduce a theme-level property (e.g., in InputDecorationTheme) that controls the horizontal alignment/padding of error/helper text independently from the input/hint text.

Example API idea:
```
InputDecorationTheme(
  errorPadding: EdgeInsets.zero, // or Alignment options
  errorAlignment: TextAlign.start,
)
```

Why it matters:

Developers often want the input/hint text to be padded, but the error text to be flush with the text field border.

This improves visual consistency in UIs.

Reduces redundancy by eliminating per-field overrides.
comments:
@YawarOsman thanks for the report. labelling it as a new proposal.

Also, **InputDecorationTheme** already has [contentPadding](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/InputDecorationTheme/contentPadding.html) property.
@YawarOsman Can you give an example of the misalignment, possibly with a repro code and screenshot? Thank you.

Issue is assigned to multiple teams (design, text-input). Please ensure the issue has only one `team-*` label at a time. Use `fyi-*` labels to have another team look at the issue without reassigning it.

> Can you give an example of the misalignment, possibly with a repro code and screenshot? Thank you. I’m hitting the same issue: errorText’s horizontal position is tied to the field’s contentPadding, so there’s no way to pad/align the error independently. **Default behavior** ```dart TextField( decoration: const InputDecoration( filled: true, border: OutlineInputBorder(), contentPadding: EdgeInsets.only(left: 20), errorText: 'Example error', ), ), ``` In this setup, the error text is always aligned with the input text and inherits the horizontal offset from contentPadding. There’s no way to customize the error text position independently. **Workaround** ```dart TextField( decoration: const InputDecoration( filled: true, border: OutlineInputBorder(), contentPadding: EdgeInsets.only(left: 0), prefix: Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.only(left: 20)), errorText: 'Example error', ), ), ``` This keeps the error text flushed to the left edge while shifting the input text using a prefix. However, it becomes brittle: it complicates further use of prefix/prefixIcon and any later contentPadding changes, because you’re constantly re-balancing offsets to avoid breaking the alignment. It would be great to have a first-class API to control the error text layout independently of the input’s contentPadding (and unaffected by prefix/suffix)—for example, an InputDecoration/InputDecorationTheme parameter like errorPadding or errorAlignment.

Without additional information, we are unfortunately not sure how to resolve this issue. We are therefore reluctantly going to close this bug for now.
If you find this problem please file a new issue with the same description, what happens, logs and the output of 'flutter doctor -v'. All system setups can be slightly different so it's always better to open new issues and reference the related ones.

issues.title: [Keyboard] Synchronize keyboard state on defocus and refocus
issues.body:
We should find ways to synchronize keyboard state when the app loses focus (maybe) and regains focus (definitely). Otherwise apps that display based on keyboard state (instead of key events) might confuse users when the app regains focus.

State:
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Linux/GTK
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Web
- [ ] Android 
- [ ] iOS 
- [ ] Fuchsia
  - Probably already done. Fuchsia sends sync/cancel events to mark key events when unfocused.
comments:
actually, syncing while the app changes focus is not enough.  
take FIGMA for example, you can zoom in/out using the keyboard + wheel even when the app is not focused.  
 

the keyboard state should also be reported along with every mouse event (like in HTML). see [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent)

Given Flutter's self expectation this issue should have a higher priority IMHO.

We recently build support for "item selection" with cmd/ctrl+click, shift+click in our app to meet users expectations on macOS, Windows and web.  Due to missing synchronization `HardwareKeyboard` wrongfully reports the meta key as being pressed when our flutter app previously lost focus via a keyboard shortcut.

This situation happens very frequently on said platforms: on the web for example this happens when a user opens a new tab with cmd/ctrl+t or opens the browser's search field with cmd+f without closing it via escape; on desktop platforms this happens whenever the user switches between applications with cmd/ctrl+tab.

Our users would return back to an app which wrongfully interprets every single click as a cmd+click. I am sure there are more "native" interactions which are blocked by this missing synchronization. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105230#issue-1258358525 has a minimal  reproducible example.

cc @timsneath @dkwingsmt 

_addendum_: I want to emphasize that is bug is **blocking us in shipping** a much requested "selection with keyboard" feature to our users.

the issue is broader than just syncing the keyboard state on defocus and refocus.

even when the app is not in focus, every mouse event should include the state of the shift/cmd/ctrl keys.

take FIGMA for example, if you use the CMD+WHEEL while the app is not in focus, the app still knows that CMD was pressed while scrolling and therefore performs ZOOM-IN/OUT instead of SCROLL.

while the app is not in focus it does not receive keyboard events and that's ok, but, it does receive mouse event and those include special keys state.
Moved to P3 based on it blocking a customer from shipping.
Could you please provide an update on this issue? We're still eager to see this picked up as it is blocking some shortcut actions in or Flutter app.

This issue is marked P1 but has had no recent status updates.

The P1 label indicates high-priority issues that are at the top of the work list. This is the highest priority level a bug can have if it isn't affecting a top-tier customer or breaking the build. Bugs marked P1 are generally actively being worked on unless the assignee is dealing with a P0 bug (or another P1 bug). Issues at this level should be resolved in a matter of months and should have monthly updates on GitHub.

Please consider where this bug really falls in our current priorities, and label it or assign it accordingly. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

I've reduced this issue to a P2 since it is not actively being worked on and has not had an actionable update in almost a year. @dkwingsmt please feel free to update if you know of plans to address this in the near term.

We've built a product using Flutter and just finished months of development. Our QA tests uncovered a serious issue: keyPress events aren't syncing or clearing correctly when the window regains focus. This bug was hard to spot during development due to the nature of our app, but it's now a major obstacle to our launch and compromises user experience.

I guess one vote is not enough to move it back to P1 but I hope more people share their stories. If such an issue was noted in the documentation I wouldn't even start the project a year ago.

Encountering same issue on flutter `Web` platform #162977 

I am actively working on production ready Accounting Software and this significantly impacts user experience and should be prioritized. Every time shortcuts stop working, the user has to close and open the app again.

Modifiers keys are removed on focus changes. I think issue only arises when trigger keys are pressed separately or along with modifier keys. I was wondering if same logic could be extended for trigger keys too.

I am trying to use `SingleActivator` more but it cannot be used for modifier keys only.
`SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.shift)` throws exception.

 Any updates from the team.

issues.title: [Android] Tapping Shift gets stuck into change-selection state
issues.body:
This is a fairly recent regression. The behavior it causes is extremely annoying when a user runs into it. After some user reports, I'd made a previous attempt to find a repro but not succeeded; thanks to @GrzegorzDyrda (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-4202898175) for pointing out Shift as the key ingredient.

### Repro
1. Open up a Flutter app on Android, with Gboard as the keyboard (e.g., a Pixel), and focus a text input.
2. Type a couple of words.
3. Tap earlier in the text input, then back at the end. The cursor will move where you tap.
4. Now tap the Shift key.
5. Again tap earlier in the text input, then back at the end.
6. **Expected:** Again the cursor moves where you tap.
7. **Actual:** A text selection opens up. One end of the selection moves where you tap; the other end remains where the cursor was. The text input remains in this state no matter how many times you tap, double-tap, etc., trying to get back to normal.

The impact is that it becomes impossible to continue editing normally in the text input. The user may have to abandon what they were typing, and navigate away from the screen and back in order to reset the whole text input from scratch.

### Discussion

The symptom is similar to #98720, though the triggering situation is different. Discussion there might have clues to a diagnosis.

There's a workaround (from a user perspective) that might give a clue to a diagnosis: once you hit the space bar, things reset to normal. (Typing other text, on the other hand, keeps the text input in the broken state: when you tap somewhere after typing, only one selection handle moves while another stays behind where you'd typed. I'd hit on this workaround a few times before by accident, but only identified the space bar as the key after @GrzegorzDyrda made the connection to #98720.) Speculating, this might be related to the fact that hitting space normally resets the state of the Shift key (unshifted vs shift vs shift-lock).

We started seeing this behavior in a release of the Zulip app which we made last month using Flutter version a567e329b090226c160c60464fba46e798973cb7 (from 2026-03-02). We hadn't seen it in previous Zulip releases, using Flutter versions up to 8cb340cd7a9b0127f8c2a4dda0523b22c3f6a54e (from 2026-01-08). Now that I have a repro, I've confirmed it's still present in the current version of Flutter main, 428e3441482b2c7f4b53c43fde0a1b8aaa8ecd1c.
comments:
I should add the Gboard version I tested this on: 17.0.9.880768217-release-arm64-v8a. That's on a Pixel 8 running Android 16.

And although the bug is clearly in Flutter, or at a minimum Flutter's problem to work around (because it doesn't seem to affect other apps), it's possible that the regression wasn't a change in Flutter: it could also have been a change in Gboard. In that case it'd be a Gboard update which just happened to roll out around the same time as the new Zulip release (with a new Flutter version) where the symptoms first appeared, about a month ago.

One good way to confirm or disconfirm that would be to take a sample app on a device where the issue reproduces with current Flutter main, and try the repro on Flutter version 8cb340cd7a9b0127f8c2a4dda0523b22c3f6a54e (or older). If it doesn't reproduce, that verifies that the regression was within Flutter. If it does still reproduce, then the regression was some external change, most likely in Gboard.
@gnprice  Thanks for the report. Labelling for `team-text-input` to investigate further.

I'm experiencing the same with both Gboard and Simple Keyboard on a Pixel 10.

The "space" workaround didn't seem to work for me, but hitting backspace does. And actually, in testing just now it looks like sometimes just hitting the shift key works to let me place the cursor normally.

Regarding the shift press I don't know if it's related, but the other frustrating thing I've noticed since this started happening (using the app Interstellar) is that the first character of a new sentence doesn't auto-capitalize. So I end a sentences with a ? or . and I expect that automatic Shift to happen, but it doesn't.

Since I experience this issue many times a day, I was forced to find some user-side workarounds myself: 1. Shift **double-tap** usually exits this sticky selection-mode. But of course it leaves the selected text in a very weird capitalization. 2. Also going to `Gboard`'s `Text editing` and tapping the `Select` button brings things back to normal (see the attached screenshot).

I have also been able to reproduce this issue.
Device:
Model: Techno SPARK 40 5G
Android Version: 15 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2db4c473-dba1-43ca-866d-a411f1fff78e

This started with a behavior change in Gboard.

Recent versions of Gboard are sending key down and up events for `KEYCODE_SHIFT_LEFT` through `InputConnection` when the user taps the on-screen shift key.

In Flutter's Android text input subsystem, `KeyEmbedderResponder` synthesizes events in order to synchronize the state of matching pairs like the left shift and right shift keys.

In this case, `KeyEmbedderResponder` handles the left shift key events by generating a key down event for the right shift key.  There is no corresponding key up event for the right shift key.

The framework therefore thinks that one of the shift keys is down.  The text selection logic then treats tap events as a shift-click that extends the selection.

@dkwingsmt

issues.title: [Android] [Google Chromecast] Unable to use software keyboard with TextField
issues.body:
The software keyboard arrow key navigation is still broken on Chromecast with Google TV devices, making text input impossible without external workarounds.

Environment:

Flutter version: 3.27.1 (also affects 3.24+)
Device: Chromecast with Google TV (4K and HD models)
Android version: Android TV 12
Input method: On-screen software keyboard
Steps to Reproduce:

Create a simple Flutter app with a TextField
Deploy to Chromecast with Google TV via adb
Navigate to the TextField using D-pad (arrow keys)
Software keyboard appears automatically
Try to use arrow keys to navigate letters on the keyboard
Expected: Arrow keys should move between keyboard letters
Actual: Arrow keys do nothing; keyboard is unresponsive
Current Workaround (NOT a solution):

The only way to make the keyboard work is to perform a hot restart:

Run the app: flutter run
Focus any TextField → keyboard shows but arrows don't work
Perform hot restart (r in terminal)
After full restart completes, keyboard arrows suddenly work
If the keyboard closes (pressing Back), the bug returns and requires another restart
Root Cause Analysis:

Based on investigation in the Flutter engine source code, the issue appears to be in InputConnectionAdaptor.java:

https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/715ede5967c8f8be259d5832da7416a316ea927f/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java#L289-L326

The InputConnectionAdaptor class intercepts arrow key events (onKeyDown/onKeyUp) and handles them in Flutter instead of returning false to let the event propagate to the Android InputMethod (software keyboard).

Theory:

When hot restart occurs, something in the lifecycle resets the input connection state, allowing the InputMethod to properly receive key events. This suggests the Flutter activity or input connection is incorrectly maintaining focus/event capture over the on-screen keyboard.

Attempted Fixes (all failed):

- ❌ Removing all Flutter shortcuts/actions from TextField
- ❌ Setting skipTraversal: false on Focus widgets
- ❌ Returning KeyEventResult.ignored for all arrow keys
- ❌ Programmatically hiding/showing keyboard via SystemChannels.textInput
- ❌ Using FocusNode listeners to detect keyboard state
- ❌ Checking AppLifecycleState changes

None of these Flutter-level solutions work because the problem is in the native Android layer (engine).

Impact:

This makes TextField input completely unusable on Chromecast devices, which represent a significant portion of Android TV users. Users cannot:

Enter email addresses for login
Type search queries
Fill forms
Enter any text input
Related Issues:

#154924 (originally reported, closed as duplicate)
[Add other related issue numbers if found]
Minimum Reproducible Example:

```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: Container(
            width: 300,
            child: TextField(
              decoration: InputDecoration(
                labelText: 'Test Input',
                border: OutlineInputBorder(),
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
Run on Chromecast:

```
adb connect <chromecast-ip>:5555
flutter run -d <device-id>
```

Expected Behavior:

Arrow keys should navigate the on-screen keyboard letters, allowing text input via remote control (standard Android TV behavior).

Actual Behavior:

Arrow keys are captured by Flutter and do nothing. The keyboard is unresponsive until a hot restart is performed.

Request:

Please investigate the InputConnectionAdaptor implementation to ensure key events are properly propagated to the Android InputMethod when a software keyboard is active on Android TV devices.

Possible Solutions:

Check InputConnection.isKeyboardActive() before handling arrow keys in Flutter
Return false from onKeyDown/onKeyUp when an InputMethod is consuming events
Add Android TV specific handling that defers to the system keyboard
Provide a flag/configuration to disable Flutter's key event interception for TextFields on TV platforms
Additional Context:

This issue specifically affects Android TV devices with software keyboards (Chromecast) but does NOT affect:

Android TV devices with physical keyboards
Android phones/tablets (touch keyboards work fine)
The hot restart workaround proves this is a state/lifecycle issue, not a fundamental incompatibility.
comments:
From triage pov: I unfortunately can't reproduce this issue as I don't have such device on my end.
However, per https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/154924#issuecomment-2372123471, this issue seems to be valid until now, as it had no bandwidth to solve at that time. Keeping this issue to have team's input.

Hello @georgehoss, as you mentioned the issue I opened last year for this bug so here is what I learned about this :

- It does not only affect Google Chromecast devices. Some Phillips or Samsung TV are impacted.
- Google can reject your app during review because of this bug.
- I tried to tweak InputConnectionAdaptor in engine but I wasn't able to fix it :(

issues.title: [android] Janky Animation when opening keyboard
issues.body:
Animating a Platform View widget is janky.

Tested on latest stable and master 3.8.0-5.0.pre.1, android 12SKQ1.211006.001, xiaomi mi lite 11  5G.

## Steps to Reproduce
Please run this simple example here:
https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo

**Expected results:**
When opening keyboard, native widget should animate smoothly. 

**Actual results:**
Native widget animation is janky. 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/53510751/218093471-c8eee90e-0b8e-491e-84a6-9f4186353c61.MP4


**Flutter doctor:**
 [✓] Flutter (Channel master, 3.8.0-5.0.pre.1, on macOS 12.2 21D49 darwin-arm64, locale en-IT) [!] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 31.0.0) ✗ cmdline-tools component is missing Run path/to/sdkmanager --install "cmdline-tools;latest" See https://developer.android.com/studio/command-line for more details. ✗ Android license status unknown. Run flutter doctor --android-licenses to accept the SDK licenses. See https://flutter.dev/docs/get-started/install/macos#android-setup for more details. [✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 13.4.1) [✓] Chrome - develop for the web [✓] Android Studio (version 2021.2) [✓] Connected device (3 available) [✓] HTTP Host Availability
comments:
Thanks for the report @delfme 
Can you please enable `performancOverlay` and see if you get shader jank while opening the keyboard ?
Also, is that the default device keyboard or external one such as Gboard ?

Thanks for the update @delfme. The similar issue you linked states that it may not be related to `platform-views` https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120555#issuecomment-1428387351.
Since this issue could also be related, although trying to S10 device, the jank I observed was  not consistent and sometimes smooth with animation and the jank wasn't noticeable mostly. 

Is it possible to confirm without adding native textfield and check if the animation is still janky on the device you used ?

https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/blob/d18841e1a0542e8fbe70dff463cade69448b1d7e/lib/demo_screen.dart#L99

I will try to look into this. Indeed it seems that issue may not be due to platform view https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120555#issuecomment-1429871820

Can you also help with this? By disabling lines 98- 
131 and enabling 134-151 we can debug the issue with flutter textfield.

https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/blob/d18841e1a0542e8fbe70dff463cade69448b1d7e/lib/demo_screen.dart#L98

I tried as above, although I see some jank in performance overlay, the animation jank while opening the keyboard is not noticeable as it opens pretty smoothly on S10 atleast I tried on.

I'll keep this issue open for team's attention and for better tracking.

@darshankawar janks/jittery occurs randomly yet consistently. On my Xiaomi Mi Lite 11 is much evident. It might not be wrong animation curve. Right now we are debugging same issue on iOS and it seems to be caused by flutter engine, we saw GC and raster getting stuck waiting. 

If flutter team can work on this, the discovery on other issue should be helpful, or else we will try to have a look at this after iOS fix

Linking here the unsync keyboard issue coz the two issues might be intertwined.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/116836

@reidbaker saw you've recently triaged https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/163516 Would you be so kind as to check if the janky / stuttering keyboard animation still persists in Flutter 3.29? This issue affects both iOS and Android and significantly impacts the user experience.
Has a solution been developed regarding this issue?
Any updates ?

issues.title: [IOS] Inconsistent Behavior of Password Save Prompt in Flutter Autofill using `TextInput.finishAutofillContext(shouldSave: true)`
comments:
@ahmtydn Thanks for the detailed report.
Please check if your issue is similar to / resembles https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/116889 or not.

Thank you for pointing this out. After reviewing issue #116889, I believe the behavior in my case is different:

- In my case, the password save prompt appears the first time `TextInput.finishAutofillContext(shouldSave: true)` is called, but subsequent calls do not trigger the prompt unless the text fields are cleared and re-filled with new input. 

- In issue #116889, the main problem seems to be that the prompt is only triggered when the input loses focus, and in some cases, it doesn’t appear at all on the first attempt. My issue doesn’t involve focus loss or the first-time prompt issue; it’s more about the prompt not appearing consistently on multiple invocations.

These differences suggest that the underlying causes might not be the same, even though both involve Autofill behavior. Let me know if more details are needed for further clarification.

Thanks for the update. I was able to replicate the reported behavior on latest sdk versions.

```
stable : 3.27.1
master : 3.28.0-2.0.pre.38699
```
/cc @LongCatIsLooong @chunhtai who are also on the original engine patch https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/57209
